assertions.assertion: Output identifies RequirePkce = true as a required control and warns against setting it to false
assertions.rationale: Section 1 identifies PKCE as the first hardening step. Shows RequirePkce = true in code and states 'Setting RequirePkce = false removes this protection and should not be done in production.'

assertions.assertion: Output identifies exact-match fully-qualified redirect URIs with no wildcards as required
assertions.rationale: Section 2 states 'Redirect URIs should be exact-match, fully-qualified HTTPS URIs' and warns that 'Wildcards in redirect URIs can be exploited to redirect authorization codes to attacker-controlled endpoints.' Shows good vs bad examples.

assertions.assertion: Output identifies restricting grant types to only what a client needs (principle of least grant) and removing implicit flow
assertions.rationale: Section 3 titled 'Use the Minimum Required Grant Types' states 'Each client should only have the grant types it actually needs' and lists correct per-client-type assignments. Explicitly states 'The implicit flow should be avoided entirely in favor of authorization code flow with PKCE.'
